description: Discovery reduces risk before committing to delivery. These discovery tracks test whether a potential solution is valuable, viable, usable, and feasible.

riskFramework.question: Will customers choose to use it or pay for it?
Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
Can customers figure out how to use it?
Can we build it within given resources and constraints?

items.title: Merchant ROI and feed reliability discovery
items.name: Merchant ROI and feed reliability discovery
items.summary: Validated that professional sellers need better feed diagnostics, lead workflow visibility, and paid exposure reporting to justify spend.
items.opportunity: Clarify which merchant workflow gaps most directly affect retention and expansion.

linkedInitiatives.description: Improve merchant retention by strengthening feed reliability, lead workflow visibility, and paid exposure reporting in the merchant workspace.

assignments.how: Owned merchant interviews, workflow synthesis, and ROI problem framing.

riskFocus.question: Will customers choose to use it or pay for it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Professional sellers will retain and expand spend when reporting and operational diagnostics are materially clearer.
evidenceToCollect: Merchant willingness to pay for better visibility products.

items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to prioritize merchant ROI reporting ahead of broader portal redesign.
items.outcome: Proceed. Evidence showed feed reliability, lead SLA visibility, and paid performance reporting were the main merchant retention blockers.

items.title: Protected transactions and fraud controls discovery
items.name: Protected transactions and fraud controls discovery
items.summary: Validated that selected high-risk categories need stronger identity, scam prevention, and optional protected payment flows.
items.opportunity: Clarify where buyer and seller trust gaps most directly suppress higher-value marketplace conversion.

linkedInitiatives.description: Strengthen identity, fraud controls, and optional protected transaction flows in higher-risk categories.

assignments.how: Owned fraud-pattern analysis and trust design recommendations.

riskFocus.question: Will customers choose to use it or pay for it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Users will accept more platform-mediated transaction steps when category value and risk are high enough.
evidenceToCollect: Buyer and seller willingness to use protected payment options.
Category-level fraud patterns and dispute rates.

items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to launch protected transaction flows in selected categories first.
items.outcome: Proceed in selected categories only. The evidence supported a targeted rollout rather than a marketplace-wide mandate.
